textPlain: I'm not quite sure I understand how they are applicable, since access patterns itself can be arbitrary complexFor example how is github.event.pull_request.labels[another_variable].name handled?

Since it only needs to classify the result as one of "fixed", "structured" or "arbitrary", it doesn't matter what the value of "another_variable" is, you'll always be in the same state at the end. Finite state transducers can handle an infinite number of possibilities with a finite number of states just fine.It's a bit strange though that the author constructs the FST to only recognize a finite list of strings. Probably some of the matching logic that could have been part of the FST is pushed into the normalizer instead.
